title: branch — model relationships
description: Branch is the FK-central aggregate of the whole platform. Outbound: 3 FKs (tenant + organization + region), all required + immutable. Inbound: every downstream domain aggregate (facility, team, staff, coach, athlete, session, booking, membership, pass, visit) references `branches.id` via `branch_id`. This is where the platform hierarchy actually joins up.

internal.note: Single-aggregate module — no internal relations within the branch module itself.

outbound.description: Home tenant. CASCADE aligns with tenancy::TenantErased — erasing a Tenant erases every Branch under it. The `BranchesOnTenantErased` hook cancels in-flight geocoding jobs before the cascade fires.

Structural sub-brand this Branch belongs to. RESTRICT on delete — an Organization cannot be dropped while any Branch references it. Organization archival is the correct path (which requires every Branch to be closed first).
Commercial + regulatory zone (currency, tax, timezone, locale). RESTRICT — Regions carry historic invoice snapshots and cannot be dropped while any Branch references them.

outbound.note: There is NO outbound FK to `applications` on Branch. Application cascades through `tenant_id → tenants.application_id` per hierarchy.md §2 + tenancy-columns.md §2 — no shortcut.

inbound.description: Every downstream domain model with a per-branch operational context composes the `BelongsToBranch` trait (this module's owned trait). They store the linkage as `branch_id` FK (non-nullable for most — a session or booking without a venue is nonsensical).
examples: facility::Facility.branch_id — required. RESTRICT: facilities block branch closure until reassigned or closed.
teams::TeamMember.branch_id — denormalised from Team for query performance; kept in sync by observer.
sports::Coach.branch_id — required for on-site coaches; nullable for federation-level coaches (Wave 3).
sports::Athlete.branch_id — required (an athlete is registered at a branch even when they participate in touring events).
sports::Session.branch_id — required. RESTRICT — sessions block branch closure.
finance::Membership.branch_id — required. The parent contract runs on the branch's Stripe account, per hierarchy.md §17.
inbound.onDelete: RESTRICT by convention on domain FKs referencing branches.id — branch closure is guarded by the observer's dependency check + the `branch.close.dependency_checks` config registry. The dependency check is enforced in application code (with a 409 BRANCH_IN_USE response) rather than by DB-level RESTRICT so that the user gets a clear error naming which downstream rows block the closure. Downstream FKs still carry RESTRICT as belt-and-braces.

accessor_chain.description: Branch surfaces convenience accessors so downstream code rarely reaches into the joined aggregates directly.

note_on_polymorphism: Every polymorphic FK targeting a Branch stores `subject_type = 'Academorix\Branch\Models\Branch'` (or the class alias `App\Branch\Branch` when Laravel's morph map is configured). Downstream modules that filter by branch on a polymorphic table use this — e.g. `activity_log.subject_type = 'Branch' AND activity_log.subject_id = brn_...`.
